pattern: Two inclusive intervals overlap exactly when each one starts no later than the other one ends, which reduces to two string comparisons since HH:MM sorts lexicographically like time.
hints: Each event is a closed interval [start, end]; you only need to decide whether the two intervals share any point.
Zero-padded HH:MM strings compare in chronological order with a plain string comparison, so you can skip parsing.
There is a conflict precisely when start1 <= end2 and start2 <= end1.

edge_inputs.description: touch at a single shared instant
adjacent but not overlapping by one minute
one event fully contains the other
full-day events both spanning midnight bounds

stress.description: random valid HH:MM intervals on both sides
